seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: input+output: generalize the forest/tree to an arbitrary connected hyperlane graph and use graph hop-distance coverage with per-node build costs, turning a poly-time tree domination into NP-hard minimum-cost r-covering (facility location).
title: Interstellar Relay Coverage: Lighting the Star Lanes
strategies: trivial: build a relay on every system (feasible, equals the baseline cost B)
coverage-greedy: repeatedly build the relay illuminating the most still-dark systems
cost-ratio greedy + redundancy pruning: pick relays maximizing new-coverage per credit, then drop redundant relays cheapest-cost-last
local search: swap/replace relays and remove dominated ones to shrink total credit spend
